new_classes.definition_en: Quality borne by a spirit that is single grain whisky: produced at a single distillery by distilling a fermented mash of malted barley with or without whole grains of other cereals, and is neither single malt Scotch whisky nor blended malt Scotch whisky.

A distillation process producing single grain or blended grain Scotch whisky at a single distillery, using a fermented mash of malted barley with or without whole grains of other cereals, occurring in Scotland.

new_classes.label_en: Single grain whisky and blended grain whisky, in containers holding > 2 l (CN 2208 30 69)
new_classes.label_de: "Single Grain"- und "Blended Grain"-Whisky in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von mehr als 2 l (KN 2208 30 69)
new_classes.definition_en: Single grain whisky or blended grain whisky — whisky produced from a fermented mash of malted barley with or without whole grains of other cereals (not exclusively malted barley), neither single malt nor blended malt Scotch whisky — presented in containers holding more than 2 litres, produced in Scotland.

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the key distinguishing criteria: (1) grain whisky type (single grain or blended grain, i.e. mash of malted barley with or without whole grains of other cereals, not exclusively malted barley), (2) produced in Scotland via the SingleGrainWhiskyDistillationProcess occurring in ScotlandSite, (3) not single malt nor blended malt Scotch whisky, (4) container volume > 2 l (LargeContainer), (5) sibling exclusions. Minor gap: the legal text's distinction between single grain (one distillery) vs blended grain (blend of single grain whiskies from multiple distilleries) is partially represented only through the SingleGrainWhiskyQuality; the blended grain sub-type is not separately modelled since both are jointly classified under 22083069. The 'predominantly' qualifier on cereal composition could not be fully formalised in OWL without a numeric property not yet present in the TBox.
